title: NLS defocusing dark soliton
ic: Psi(x,0) = tanh(x). (Dark soliton, density |Psi|^2 = tanh^2(x) has a zero at x=0 — this stress-tests N->0 handling.)
ground_truth: Stationary dark soliton, density profile tanh^2(x) preserved exactly. Phase jumps by pi across x=0.
stress_question: How does Madelung-Psi handle the N=0 point at x=0? Does direct (N, phi) blow up? What regularization is needed? Are periodic-vs-Dirichlet boundary conditions an issue?
parameters_to_sweep: Try Madelung-Psi (with phase jump) and direct (N, phi) (with regularization N->max(N, eps), eps in {1e-6, 1e-4, 1e-2}).
